text: I work at the SPF Group.
I need a new server for the project.
She is an experienced IT manager.
Stress
Rhythm
Syllable
Intonation
Vowel
Consonant
Natural
Pronounce
Definite
The IT department is on the third floor.
I need a new laptop for the project.
The conference starts at nine.
I work at the SPF Group. It is a large distributor in Brazil.
Daniela, the conference starts at nine on Monday, right?
Yes. It is an important event for the whole IT department.
And the new server? Do we have a decision yet?
Not yet. We need a final answer today. The vendor is sending an engineer.
Good. Where is the meeting?
The room on the third floor is busy, so we use the room next to reception.
Perfect. One last thing, how do you pronounce the vendor name?
It is Novatech, the stress is on the second syllable.

Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, a short tip about two things that make you sound natural: articles and rhythm. First, articles. Use a or an for something general, mentioned for the first time. I need a new laptop. She is an experienced manager. Use the when both people know exactly which one. The conference starts at nine. Remember, use an before a vowel sound. An alert, an hour, an IT manager. Now, rhythm. English is a stress language. We push the important words and we relax the small ones. Listen. I work at the S P F Group. The strong words are work and group. The article the becomes very short and quiet. Try to feel the beat. In the word implement, the stress falls on the first part. In technology, push the middle. So, two small habits. Choose the right article, and let the strong words ring while the small words stay soft. Practice with one sentence about your job, and feel the rhythm. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus from the office. I am leaving you a quick voicemail about the schedule. So, the conference starts at nine on Monday. It is an important event for the whole IT department. We need a final decision about the new server today. The vendor is sending a technician, and she is an engineer with a lot of experience. Also, a small thing. The meeting room on the third floor is busy, so we will use the room next to reception instead. One more point. I noticed a glitch in the report, but it was a minor one, nothing serious. When you have a moment, please confirm the agenda for the team. Thanks, Daniela. Talk soon.
